applicationInfo.name: Greeting — CLI + Cloudflare Worker
applicationInfo.description: A time-aware colourised terminal greeting CLI with RFC 865 Quote of the Day service, async SQLite telemetry, and a Cloudflare Worker HTTP API backed by D1. Live demo: https://greeting-worker.cancun.workers.dev/

architecture.description:
## Components

- **src/greeting.py** — Greeting class: reads system clock, classifies hour via TimeOfDay enum, prints colourised greeting + optional quote to stdout.
- **src/time_of_day.py** — TimeOfDay enum: period classification (Morning/Afternoon/Evening/Night), colorama colours, salutations.
- **src/quote_provider.py** — QuoteProvider: 120-quote RFC 865 corpus, shuffle-deck selection, optional seeding.
- **src/quote_service.py** — QuoteService: RFC 865 TCP + UDP server (configurable port, default 127.0.0.1).
- **src/telemetry.py** — TelemetryStore: async SQLite writer (background queue thread), nanosecond precision (duration_ns INTEGER).
- **src/stats.py** — StatsReporter: min/max/mean/median/p95/p99 from telemetry DB.
- **worker/src/entry.py** — Cloudflare Worker HTTP API (GET /, /quote, /stats; POST /reset).
- **worker/src/cf_telemetry.py** — D1-backed telemetry adapter (JsProxy row handling).
- **.github/workflows/ci.yml** — GitHub Actions CI: multi-Python matrix, pip-audit CVE scan, Sphinx docs build.

dataflow.description:
## Data Flows

1. **CLI path**: __main__ -> Greeting.__init__(name) -> Greeting.build(now) -> TimeOfDay.from_hour() -> QuoteProvider.get() -> stdout. Telemetry recorded async to SQLite via background queue.
2. **TCP/UDP path**: Network client -> QuoteService._handle_tcp() / UDP loop -> QuoteProvider.get() -> socket response bytes.
3. **Worker HTTP path**: Browser -> Cloudflare edge -> Default.fetch() -> _build_greeting() -> QuoteProvider.get() + CfTelemetry.record() -> D1 database -> HTML/JSON response.
4. **Stats path**: GET /stats -> CfTelemetry.historic_summary() -> D1 SELECT -> _rows_to_python() -> JSON response.
5. **CI/CD path**: git push -> GitHub Actions -> pip install -> unittest -> pip-audit -> sphinx-build.

assumptions.content: The CLI runs on a trusted developer workstation; the OS clock is not adversarially controlled.
QuoteService is bound to 127.0.0.1 by default and is not exposed to public networks unless explicitly configured.
The SQLite telemetry database resides on a local filesystem accessible only to the running user.
The Cloudflare Worker runs in an isolated V8 isolate; D1 access is mediated by the Cloudflare platform.
No secrets (API tokens, database IDs) are committed to the repository; wrangler.local.toml is gitignored.
GitHub Actions runners are ephemeral and isolated; repository secrets are managed via GitHub Secrets.

mitigations.content: Strip ANSI escape sequences and non-printable characters from name input; enforce 100-character max length at Greeting construction time.
Validate hour is an integer in range 0-23 inside TimeOfDay.from_hour(); raise ValueError on out-of-range input.
Pin all PyPI dependencies with SHA-256 hashes using pip-compile --generate-hashes; use --require-hashes in CI.
Run pip-audit on every CI build with --disable-pip --no-deps to scan for known CVEs in dependencies.
Pin all GitHub Actions to full commit SHAs instead of mutable version tags to prevent tag-hijacking supply chain attacks.
Declare minimal permissions (contents: read) at workflow and job level in ci.yml; never grant write unless required.
Require PR approval before CI runs on first-time fork contributors; never echo secrets in workflow steps.
Require signed commits and enforce via branch protection; enable Dependabot security alerts and version updates.
Use PyPI Trusted Publishers (OIDC) for publishing; sign release artifacts with sigstore; enable 2FA on PyPI account.
QuoteProvider validates quote length <= 512 chars at construction; apply same ANSI-stripping logic for custom corpora.
Implement a bounded thread pool (ThreadPoolExecutor) with max_workers cap and per-IP connection rate limiting for QuoteService.
Catch all socket errors in QuoteService handlers; log at WARNING level server-side only; never send stack traces to clients.
Default QuoteService host to 127.0.0.1 (loopback); document that 0.0.0.0 must only be used in trusted network environments.
Use CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE or authbind/systemd socket activation instead of running as root to bind port 17.
Truncate error_msg to 500 characters before writing to SQLite; set DB file permissions to 600 (owner read/write only).
Wrap db_path in pathlib.Path() at TelemetryStore construction to normalise traversal sequences; validate path is within expected directory if exposed to untrusted config.
Store all Cloudflare credentials (API token, database_id) in gitignored wrangler.local.toml and environment variables only; never commit to repository.
Sanitise name input in Worker entry.py using the same two-pass ANSI + non-printable strip as the CLI; enforce 100-char max.

threats.statement: An external caller with access to the Greeting API, passing unsanitised input, can inject ANSI escape sequences or control characters into the name parameter, which leads to terminal manipulation or display corruption, negatively impacting the integrity of terminal output.
threats.threatSource: external caller with access to the Greeting API
threats.threatAction: inject ANSI escape sequences or control characters into the name parameter

threats.statement: A developer or test harness with the ability to call Greeting.build(), passing a crafted datetime object, can supply an out-of-range hour value, which leads to incorrect period classification, negatively impacting greeting correctness.
threats.threatSource: developer or test harness
threats.prerequisites: with the ability to call Greeting.build()
threats.threatAction: supply an out-of-range hour value via a crafted datetime object

threats.statement: An external caller with access to the Greeting API, passing an unbounded string, can supply a multi-megabyte name value, which leads to excessive memory allocation during f-string construction, negatively impacting process stability.
threats.threatSource: external caller with access to the Greeting API
threats.threatAction: supply a multi-megabyte name value
threats.threatImpact: excessive memory allocation during f-string construction

threats.statement: A local attacker with OS-level access, manipulating the system clock, can cause datetime.now() to return a falsified time, which leads to an incorrect time-of-day greeting and colour, negatively impacting output accuracy.
threats.threatSource: local attacker with OS-level access
threats.threatAction: cause datetime.now() to return a falsified time

threats.statement: A supply chain attacker with control over a PyPI package release, compromising the colorama package, can execute arbitrary code at import time in the developer or CI environment, which leads to full environment compromise, negatively impacting source code integrity and CI pipeline security.
threats.threatSource: supply chain attacker with control over a PyPI package release
threats.threatAction: execute arbitrary code at import time in the developer or CI environment

threats.statement: A malicious package author with a typosquatted package name on PyPI, exploiting developer typing errors, can cause installation of a malicious package instead of colorama, which leads to arbitrary code execution, negatively impacting the developer workstation and CI environment.
threats.threatSource: malicious package author with a typosquatted package name on PyPI
threats.threatAction: cause installation of a malicious package instead of colorama

threats.statement: A malicious contributor with the ability to submit a pull request, injecting commands into workflow steps, can execute arbitrary code on the GitHub Actions runner, which leads to CI runner compromise and secret exfiltration, negatively impacting the CI/CD pipeline and repository integrity.
threats.threatSource: malicious contributor with the ability to submit a pull request
threats.threatAction: execute arbitrary code on the GitHub Actions runner
threats.threatImpact: CI runner compromise and secret exfiltration

threats.statement: A compromised GitHub Actions maintainer with control over a mutable version tag, pushing malicious code to an existing tag, can silently replace a trusted action used in ci.yml, which leads to arbitrary code execution on the CI runner, negatively impacting pipeline integrity.
threats.threatSource: compromised GitHub Actions maintainer with control over a mutable version tag
threats.prerequisites: pushing malicious code to an existing tag
threats.threatAction: silently replace a trusted action used in ci.yml
threats.threatImpact: arbitrary code execution on the CI runner

threats.statement: A workflow with excessive default permissions, triggered by a push or pull_request event, can write to repository contents without explicit authorisation, which leads to unintended repository modifications, negatively impacting source code integrity.
threats.threatSource: workflow with excessive default permissions
threats.prerequisites: triggered by a push or pull_request event
threats.threatAction: write to repository contents without explicit authorisation

threats.statement: A dependency confusion attacker with a malicious package on a public registry, exploiting pip resolution order, can substitute a private package name with a malicious public one during CI install, which leads to arbitrary code execution in the CI environment, negatively impacting pipeline and build integrity.
threats.threatSource: dependency confusion attacker with a malicious package on a public registry
threats.threatAction: substitute a private package name with a malicious public one during CI install
threats.threatImpact: arbitrary code execution in the CI environment

metadata.value: CICD-SEC-8: Ungoverned Usage of 3rd Party Services

threats.statement: A misconfigured workflow step with access to environment variables, echoing secrets in a run block, can expose tokens or credentials in public CI logs, which leads to secret exfiltration, negatively impacting Cloudflare API token and repository secret confidentiality.
threats.threatSource: misconfigured workflow step with access to environment variables
threats.prerequisites: echoing secrets in a run block
threats.threatAction: expose tokens or credentials in public CI logs

threats.statement: A compromised contributor with commit access, silently modifying requirements.txt or setup.py, can add a malicious dependency without triggering an automated review, which leads to undetected supply chain compromise, negatively impacting downstream consumers and the CI pipeline.
threats.threatSource: compromised contributor with commit access
threats.threatAction: add a malicious dependency without triggering an automated review

threats.statement: An attacker with compromised PyPI account credentials, publishing a malicious release without a signature, can distribute tampered package artifacts to downstream consumers, which leads to arbitrary code execution for anyone installing the package, negatively impacting package consumers.
threats.threatSource: attacker with compromised PyPI account credentials
threats.prerequisites: publishing a malicious release without a signature
threats.threatAction: distribute tampered package artifacts to downstream consumers
threats.threatImpact: arbitrary code execution for anyone installing the package

threats.statement: A caller with the ability to supply a custom quotes tuple to QuoteProvider, injecting ANSI escape sequences, can cause terminal manipulation on the receiving TCP/UDP client, which leads to corrupted terminal state, negatively impacting QuoteService clients.
threats.threatSource: caller with the ability to supply a custom quotes tuple to QuoteProvider
threats.threatAction: cause terminal manipulation on the receiving TCP/UDP client

threats.statement: A caller with the ability to instantiate QuoteProvider, passing a very large quotes tuple, can cause excessive memory allocation during corpus construction, which leads to process-level memory exhaustion, negatively impacting application availability.
threats.threatSource: caller with the ability to instantiate QuoteProvider
threats.prerequisites: passing a very large quotes tuple
threats.threatAction: cause excessive memory allocation during corpus construction

threats.statement: A network attacker with the ability to open TCP connections, opening many simultaneous connections to QuoteService, can exhaust the server file descriptor limit or thread pool, which leads to service unavailability, negatively impacting all QuoteService clients.
threats.threatSource: network attacker with the ability to open TCP connections
threats.prerequisites: opening many simultaneous connections to QuoteService
threats.threatAction: exhaust the server file descriptor limit or thread pool

threats.statement: An attacker with access to server logs, triggering unhandled exceptions in QuoteService socket handlers, can read stack traces revealing internal paths and library versions, which leads to information disclosure, negatively impacting operational security.
threats.threatSource: attacker with access to server logs
threats.prerequisites: triggering unhandled exceptions in QuoteService socket handlers
threats.threatAction: read stack traces revealing internal paths and library versions
threats.threatImpact: information disclosure of internal implementation details

threats.statement: A misconfigured deployment with QuoteService bound to 0.0.0.0, exposing the service on all network interfaces, can allow any internet client to connect and consume quotes or launch DoS attacks, which leads to unintended public exposure, negatively impacting service availability and confidentiality.
threats.threatSource: misconfigured deployment with QuoteService bound to 0.0.0.0
threats.prerequisites: exposing the service on all network interfaces
threats.threatAction: allow any internet client to connect and consume quotes or launch DoS attacks
threats.threatImpact: unintended public exposure of the quote service

threats.statement: A developer running QuoteService as root to bind port 17, with a vulnerability in the service, can allow an attacker to achieve root-level code execution, which leads to full system compromise, negatively impacting the entire host system.
threats.threatSource: developer running QuoteService as root to bind port 17
threats.prerequisites: with a vulnerability in the service
threats.threatAction: allow an attacker to achieve root-level code execution

threats.statement: A network attacker with the ability to spoof UDP source addresses, sending spoofed datagrams to QuoteService, can redirect quote responses to a victim IP, which leads to a UDP amplification/reflection attack, negatively impacting the victim network availability.
threats.threatSource: network attacker with the ability to spoof UDP source addresses
threats.threatAction: redirect quote responses to a victim IP
threats.threatImpact: UDP amplification/reflection attack against a victim

threats.statement: A buggy or malicious caller with the ability to invoke TelemetryStore.record(), passing an unbounded error string, can write multi-megabyte payloads to the SQLite error_msg column, which leads to disk exhaustion and potential information leakage, negatively impacting local storage and operational security.
threats.threatSource: buggy or malicious caller with the ability to invoke TelemetryStore.record()
threats.threatAction: write multi-megabyte payloads to the SQLite error_msg column
threats.threatImpact: disk exhaustion and potential information leakage

threats.statement: A caller with the ability to configure TelemetryStore, supplying a crafted db_path value, can cause sqlite3.connect() to open an unexpected file or URI, which leads to unintended file access or database confusion, negatively impacting data integrity.
threats.threatSource: caller with the ability to configure TelemetryStore
threats.threatAction: cause sqlite3.connect() to open an unexpected file or URI
threats.threatImpact: unintended file access or database confusion
